steps: Install Docker on your machine if not already installed
Pull and run the Gotenberg container: docker run --rm -p 3000:3000 gotenberg/gotenberg:8
The API will be available at http://localhost:3000
For production, deploy behind a reverse proxy (nginx, Caddy, Traefik) and add authentication if needed
Update the base_url in your gateway configuration to point to your Gotenberg instance (e.g., http://localhost:3000)
notes: Gotenberg is entirely self-hosted — there is no SaaS version or API key to obtain
The base_url must be replaced with your own instance URL (default: http://localhost:3000)
No built-in authentication; add a reverse proxy with auth if the instance is publicly accessible
Supports Chromium (HTML/URL/Markdown to PDF/screenshot) and LibreOffice (Office docs to PDF)
A hosted demo is available at https://api.gotenberg.dev but is rate-limited and not for production

test_endpoint.description: Check the health of the Gotenberg instance and verify it is running
